_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-local autopilot, Adam-supervised)
_meta.note: LOCAL refinement batch — VA: Rick West (Mayor of Chesapeake R), Andy Wolfenbarger (FXBG School Board Ward 1), Annie Langdon (FXBG School Board Ward 3), Anton Bell (Hampton Commonwealth's Attorney D), Carolyn Campbell (Hampton City Council). Roster verified June 2026. All TRUE/FALSE cite a source URL; null where no evidence. Prior speculative party-default cells reset to null.

profile.education: Great Bridge HS (1970); Old Dominion University B.S. Secondary Education (1974); University of Virginia M.S. Educational Administration & Leadership (1979); Virginia Tech Doctorate in Leadership and Policy Studies (1998)
profile.background: Dr. Richard W. 'Rick' West. Native rural Chesapeake; Navy father. 30+ year career in Chesapeake City Public Schools as teacher and principal (Southeastern Elementary, Great Bridge Intermediate, Hickory MS, Greenbrier MS). Served on Chesapeake City Council May 2008–Nov 2017 (re-elected 2012, 2016). Sworn in as Mayor of Chesapeake Nov 14, 2017 (filled unexpired term); won special election May 2018; re-elected May 2020; re-elected Nov 5, 2024 over Councilman Don Carey (56%–43.9%). Current term ends Dec 31, 2028. Regional service: HRTAC, HRTPO, Hampton Roads Military & Federal Facilities Alliance, Eastern VA Regional Industrial Facility Authority. No public church/denominational affiliation publicly stated.

rick-west.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Mayor of Chesapeake since Nov 14, 2017; re-elected Nov 5, 2024 over Councilman Don Carey 56-44; current term ends 12/31/2028. Republican; endorsed and funded by Republican Party of Chesapeake. HARD evidence: (1) issued the FIRST-EVER Chesapeake mayoral Pride Month proclamation (signed May 28, 2024 / acknowledged at the May 28 council meeting, effective June 2024) stating 'LGBTQIA+ individuals have had immeasurable impact to the cultural, civic and economic successes of our community' and encouraging residents to recognize the Stonewall uprising — biblical_marriage q4 FALSE and christian_liberty q4 FALSE (affirmative state-actor use of municipal letterhead to honor LGBTQ+ ideology); (2) presided over and signed the unanimous 9-0 FY24 budget that CUT the real-estate tax rate 4 cents ($1.05 → $1.01) — economic_stewardship q2 TRUE; (3) explicit campaign pledge in 'Issues' to deliver 'significant pay raises for our public safety professionals, preventing staff shortages'; publicly nominated 23-year Chesapeake PD Capt. Jeff Jefferies to fill the council vacancy 9/23/2025; framed his post-Holly-Cove-shooting response as continuing to work with Chesapeake police — public_justice q0 TRUE (affirmative-advocacy bar met with concrete pay-raise commitment + LE personnel nomination); (4) on Chesapeake City Council May 9, 2017 when the body voted 8-1 to adopt the substitute motion affirming Chesapeake 'shall continue to enforce federal immigration laws and regulations and shall not adopt sanctuary city policies' (dissenter publicly identified as Councilmember Ella Ward, not West) — border_immigration q2 TRUE; (5) was mayor presiding Dec 10, 2019 over the UNANIMOUS 2A 'Constitutional City' declaration responding to anticipated VA red-flag / magazine-limit / AWB legislation; quoted on the record: 'I believe you will leave here tonight with confidence that your council will do everything within our powers to preserve the rights granted to us by both the United States Constitution and the Constitution of Virginia' — self_defense q1 TRUE. DEVIATION FLAGS: (a) The Pride proclamation is the single largest Christian-conservative-rubric deviation in this profile and overrides partisan-default assumptions; (b) voted YES on Feb 2026 5-4 toll-rate increase on Chesapeake Expressway / Dominion Boulevard Veterans Bridge (with Bunn, Ritter, Jefferies, Smith YES; Ward, Newins, King, Whitaker NO) — fiscal-stewardship counter-signal; (c) during COVID publicly encouraged residents to mask, distance, and get vaccinated — refuse_state_overreach q2 FALSE (aligned with state directives rather than protecting citizens from them). NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage q0/q2, family_child_sovereignty (30-year CCPS administrator with only generic 'quality education' rhetoric — too vague), christian_liberty q0/q2/q3 (no documented Mayor-West-personally-delivered invocation in reviewed sources; he has said 'Chesapeake is a city where faith leaders lead' generically; no church publicly identified), election_integrity, public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4, refuse_state_overreach q0/q1/q3/q4, border_immigration q1, self_defense q0.

4.note: Signed and issued Chesapeake's first-ever mayoral Pride Month proclamation (May/June 2024) stating 'LGBTQIA+ individuals have had immeasurable impact to the cultural, civic and economic successes of our community' and encouraging recognition of the Stonewall uprising — affirmative municipal-letterhead promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in public policy.

Issued Chesapeake's first-ever official mayoral Pride Month proclamation — state-funded (city-letterhead) promotion of a non-Christian moral/identity framework using municipal symbolic resources; no comparable Christian-symbol / Sabbath / National Day of Prayer mayoral act documented under his signature.

2.note: Presided over and signed unanimous 9-0 FY24 Chesapeake budget that CUT the real-estate tax rate 4 cents ($1.05 → $1.01) — affirmative tax cut. DEVIATION: also voted YES on Feb 2026 5-4 toll-rate increase (flagged in notes).

0.note: Explicit campaign 'Issues' pledge to deliver 'significant pay raises for our public safety professionals, preventing staff shortages'; publicly nominated 23-year Chesapeake PD Capt. Jeff Jefferies to fill the council vacancy 9/23/2025; post-Holly-Cove-shooting statement committed to continuing work with Chesapeake police — affirmative pro-LE advocacy bar met with concrete pay-raise commitment + LE-personnel nomination.

2.note: During COVID, Mayor West publicly encouraged Chesapeake residents to wear masks, social-distance, and get vaccinated in official city videos — aligned with state public-health directives rather than protecting citizens from them.

On Chesapeake City Council May 9, 2017 when the body voted 8-1 to adopt the substitute motion affirming Chesapeake 'shall continue to enforce federal immigration laws and regulations and shall not adopt sanctuary city policies'; the lone dissenter has been publicly identified as Councilmember Ella Ward, so West voted with the 8-member anti-sanctuary majority.

1.note: Presided as mayor at the Dec 10, 2019 UNANIMOUS Chesapeake council vote declaring the city a Second Amendment 'Constitutional City' in response to anticipated VA red-flag / magazine-limit / AWB legislation; quoted on the record: 'I believe you will leave here tonight with confidence that your council will do everything within our powers to preserve the rights granted to us by both the United States Constitution and the Constitution of Virginia.'

profile.education: Doctorate in Education Leadership (VCU); M.Ed. (George Mason University)
profile.background: Andrew M. 'Andy' Wolfenbarger; 27+ year career educator, currently Director of Infrastructure and Engineering at Loudoun County Public Schools (third-largest VA division — 41 staff, tech infrastructure for ~82,500 students across 100 sites). Prior roles: 6 years high-school classroom teacher in Orange County (VA), 13 years Stafford County tech leadership, 4 years Prince William County (information security + student data). Adjunct at Germanna CC and University of Mary Washington. Lives in Fredericksburg's Idlewild neighborhood 20+ years; 8 years on the Idlewild HOA Board; three children in/through FCPS. Elected to FXBG City School Board Ward 1 Nov 4, 2025 uncontested with 1,859 votes (97.03%), filling the seat of Matt Rowe (ran for City Council Ward 1); sworn in Jan 2026; 4-year term ends Dec 2029. Race officially nonpartisan; endorsed by the Fredericksburg Democratic Committee. No public church/denominational affiliation located (the 'Pastor Andrew Wolfenbarger' on YouTube/LinkedIn is a different person, Joshua Baptist Church, Joshua, TX).

andy-wolfenbarger.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: FXBG City School Board Ward 1, elected Nov 4, 2025 uncontested (1,859 votes, 97.03%), sworn in Jan 2026, term ends Dec 2029. Race officially nonpartisan; endorsed by the Fredericksburg Democratic Committee (FXBG Republicans do not endorse in local races). Career education-establishment insider — Director at Loudoun County Public Schools, Ed.D. + M.Ed., 27 years K-12. CAMPAIGN POSTURE: deliberately performance/data-focused, NOT culture-war framing in either direction — campaign priorities are SOL scores, staff retention, academic rigor. FXBG Advance questionnaire contained NO LGBTQ-related questions and he made no public statements on Pride/drag/library content/SOGI/CRT/parental-notification. First documented vote (March 3, 2026): YES on $68.4M FY27 budget (4-1 majority, 5.7% increase) and publicly advocated for HIGHER teacher raises (9% vs proposed 7%) — a pro-spending direction, framed by him as 'fiscal responsibility... if there are big increases, it's clear to everybody.' Net read: VERY THIN ISSUE FOOTPRINT — ~5 months on the dais and deliberately avoided culture-war positioning. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (no Pride/drag/library/SOGI statement; FCPS division handles transgender students case-by-case with no formal parental-notification policy, but Wolfenbarger has not personally voted on this), family_child_sovereignty (no school-choice/parental-notification/CRT-opposition statements; Dem-endorsed but did not run on SOGI-inclusion either), christian_liberty (no invocation; no church publicly identified), economic_stewardship q2 (FY27 yes-vote + push for higher raises is a pro-spending direction but local school boards do not deficit-spend in the constitutional-balanced-budget-amendment sense the rubric question contemplates — score left null pending a more direct fiscal-restraint signal), election_integrity, public_justice (no SRO/school-safety statement), refuse_state_overreach (no VDOE-mandate statement personally), border_immigration, self_defense.

profile.background: Annie Langdon; 2002 James Monroe HS graduate; ~6–7 years Algebra teacher at JMHS (Fredericksburg City Schools), later an instructional coach; currently teaches at Dixon-Smith Middle School in Stafford County Public Schools. Has described FCPS as 'part of her life for the past 25 years' (student, teacher, parent). Mother of four (three through FCPS elementary). Elected to FXBG City School Board Ward 3 Nov 4, 2025 over Sarah Stelmok 1,609 to 954 (~62%); sworn in Jan 2026; 4-year term ends Dec 2029. Filled seat vacated by 9-year incumbent Jennifer Boyd. Race officially nonpartisan; endorsed by the Fredericksburg Democratic Committee; iVoterGuide classified her 'Liberal (Conditional)' (she did not answer iVoterGuide's questions). No public religious affiliation located.

annie-langdon.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: FXBG City School Board Ward 3, elected Nov 4, 2025 (62% over Sarah Stelmok), sworn in Jan 2026, term ends Dec 2029. Race officially nonpartisan; endorsed by Fredericksburg Democratic Committee; iVoterGuide rates 'Liberal (Conditional)' but she declined to answer their 29-question candidate survey. KEY READING NOTE: search-engine summaries that surfaced 'drag shows / chemical castration' language about her were quoting iVoterGuide's STANDARD QUESTION PROMPTS, NOT Langdon's answers — she did not answer those questions. CAMPAIGN POSTURE: framed around accountability ('we don't hold anybody to a high standard') and special-education law training for teachers. ONE DOCUMENTED VOTE: she was the LONE NO vote on the March 3, 2026 FY27 $68.4M budget (4-1) — but she dissented FROM THE LEFT, explicitly saying she wasn't satisfied with the teacher/staff salary increase (wanted higher spending on staff) — economic_stewardship q2 FALSE under the rubric's plain reading (dissent in the pro-spending direction is opposite of opposing deficit/wasteful spending). She also made the motion for FXBG's updated public-comment policy (April 14, 2026; passed unanimously); critics characterized it as chilling public comment, she framed it as keeping meetings focused on students/staff/families — not scored. FLAGS (unscored): Bryan Stelmok (husband of opponent Sarah Stelmok) publicly accused her in Feb 2026 of sharing his child's educational placement / confidential info during the 2025 campaign; she declined to comment — unresolved single-source allegation, do not score but flag. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (no Pride/drag/library/SOGI statement; FCPS division handles transgender students case-by-case with no formal parental-notification policy, but Langdon has cast no individual vote on this), family_child_sovereignty (did not answer iVoterGuide parental-records question; no school-choice/CRT/SOGI statement), christian_liberty (FCPS does not open with prayer; no faith affiliation publicly identified; declined iVoterGuide spiritual-values question), election_integrity (declined iVoterGuide photo-ID question), public_justice (no SRO/school-safety statement; declined iVoterGuide discipline question), refuse_state_overreach (no VDOE-mandate or pandemic statement; declined iVoterGuide on USDOE elimination), border_immigration, self_defense (declined iVoterGuide armed-faculty question).

2.note: Lone NO vote on the March 3, 2026 FY27 $68.4M FXBG school-board budget (4-1) — she dissented FROM THE LEFT, explicitly stating she wasn't satisfied with the teacher/staff salary increase (wanted higher spending on staff). Opposite direction from opposing deficit/wasteful local spending.

profile.religion: Ordained minister at Calvary Revival Church (Hampton); serves with wife Dr. Dawn Yvette Bell in youth ministries
profile.education: Norfolk Catholic High School; B.A. Political Science, Norfolk State University; J.D. (with Honors), North Carolina Central School of Law
profile.background: Anton A. Bell; first African-American elected Hampton Commonwealth's Attorney. Career career prosecutor — handled violent crimes, homicides, and child molestation in Hampton; served as Special Assistant U.S. Attorney (E.D. Va.) on the Peninsula Drug Task Force. Appointed CA 2012 after retirement of Linda Curtis; won the 2012 special election and was re-elected in 2013, 2017 (over Nicholas Hobbs), 2021 (unopposed), and 2023 (unopposed); current term Jan 1, 2024 – Dec 31, 2027; next election November 2027. Currently serving as Immediate Past President of the Virginia Association of Commonwealth's Attorneys (VACA), 2025–26. Married to Dr. Dawn Yvette Bell.

anton-bell.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting Hampton Commonwealth's Attorney since 2012; re-elected unopposed 2021 and 2023; current term Jan 1, 2024 – Dec 31, 2027; next election Nov 2027. Currently VACA Immediate Past President 2025-26. CROSS-PRESSURED PROFILE — do not flatten. (A) Bell is a founding member of Virginia Progressive Prosecutors for Justice (VPPFJ, formed July 2020 with 10 other VA CAs including Descano, Dehghani-Tafti, Biberaj) and signed the March 8, 2021 VPPFJ letter to the General Assembly endorsing decarceration-oriented reforms (mandatory-minimum repeal, expanded expungement); Progressive Voters Guide of Virginia 2021 endorsed him as the 'progressive choice... due to his support of criminal reform and police accountability' — public_justice q1 FALSE (coalitional alignment with the rogue-prosecutor / soft-on-mandatory-minimums archetype the rubric singles out). (B) AT THE SAME TIME he is an ordained Calvary Revival Church minister who invoked the Sixth Commandment ('Thou shalt not kill') in court argument during the Bigsby (Codi Bigsby) sentencing and publicly rejected mercy, seeking the maximum sentence — public_justice q4 TRUE (magistrate's God-ordained sword-bearing duty publicly affirmed in explicit biblical-magistrate terms — a rare and significant single data point) AND public_justice q3 TRUE (explicitly refused to frame violent crime in public-health terms; sought max sentence). (C) Office partners visibly with Hampton PD on violent crime, gang, and gun prosecution; bio describes him as a hard prosecutor; no Soros / Justice & Public Safety PAC / Real Justice PAC funding has been located (VPAP shows locally-funded committee) — public_justice q0 TRUE (affirmative pro-LE record meeting the bar without being the Soros archetype). (D) Office runs a victim-services FAQ and Bell explicitly argued for mercy to the victim's family at Bigsby sentencing — public_justice q2 TRUE. (E) Open public Christian profession from his elected-office platform (Sixth Commandment in open court, ordained minister bio on city site) — christian_liberty q0 TRUE. NOT a Soros-funded DA. Bolts noted Bell gave 'a more qualified answer than his colleagues' on parole reinstatement — i.e., he's the most cautious of the 11 VPPFJ members. DID NOT sign the 2022 FJP post-Dobbs abortion-non-prosecution joint statement (no VA prosecutor signed it). NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life (Catholic high school + ordained Calvary Revival minister biography suggests possible personal pro-life lean but no public position statement located — null per evidence rule), biblical_marriage (bio describes 'Husband, dad, servant leader' but no marriage/sexuality policy statements), family_child_sovereignty (publicly identifies family breakdown as a primary crime driver, but that does NOT map to universal school choice / parental notification / CRT — null), christian_liberty q2/q3/q4 (open public profession yes, but no specific conscience-rights / Christian-symbols / non-Christian-display statements), economic_stewardship, election_integrity, refuse_state_overreach, border_immigration (Hampton city formally cooperates with ICE and rejected the sanctuary label in 2025 but that was Mayor Gray's statement, not Bell's — no individual position located), self_defense (hard on illegal gun violence but no defensive-firearm-owner statements).

0.note: Ordained Calvary Revival Church minister; publicly invoked the Sixth Commandment ('Thou shalt not kill') in court argument during the Bigsby sentencing, framing his prosecutorial role in explicit biblical terms — affirmative public profession of Christ from elected office.

Office partners with Hampton PD on violent-crime, gang, and gun prosecution; career prosecutor of homicides, child molestation, and Peninsula Drug Task Force narcotics cases (SAUSA, E.D. Va.); no documented soft-on-LE position. Affirmative pro-LE record meeting the bar.

1.note: Founding member of Virginia Progressive Prosecutors for Justice (formed July 2020 with Descano, Dehghani-Tafti, Biberaj et al.); signed the March 8, 2021 VPPFJ letter to the VA General Assembly endorsing mandatory-minimum repeal and expanded expungement; Progressive Voters Guide of Virginia 2021 named him the 'progressive choice... due to his support of criminal reform and police accountability' — coalitional alignment with the rogue-prosecutor / soft-on-mandatory-minimums policy archetype the rubric singles out. Note: not Soros-funded and more cautious than his VPPFJ colleagues.

2.note: Office maintains a victim-services FAQ on hampton.gov/3739; at Bigsby sentencing Bell explicitly argued for mercy to the victim's family and sought the maximum sentence — direct victim-advocacy + proportional-punishment posture.

3.note: Bigsby case: publicly rejected mercy and sought maximum sentence; bio frames violent-crime prosecution in hard-enforcement terms — does NOT frame violent crime in a public-health excuse-for-diminished-punishment posture.

4.note: Invoked the Sixth Commandment ('Thou shalt not kill') in court argument during the Bigsby sentencing — explicit public framing of the prosecutor's role as the God-ordained magistrate bearing the sword (Rom. 13:4).

profile.religion: Member, Gloria Dei Lutheran Church (Hampton) since 1996; served as church council president 2012–2015; Sunday School teacher
profile.education: Associate's degree, Virginia Peninsula Community College (1984); B.S. Computer Science, Virginia Tech (1986)
profile.background: Hampton native; Hampton-police-officer's daughter. IT/software career — Newport News Shipbuilding, Wyle Labs, SAIC — before founding Cornerstone Investors Inc. (real-estate investment firm) in 2007. Married John Campbell 1987; two adult sons. Member, Lafayette Gun Club Inc. Civic: Habitat for Humanity volunteer since 1996, Youth Sailing VA, Impact 100 Greater Peninsula, Hampton Yacht Club, Boy Scouts youth leader. Signed up as Officer of Election and joined Hampton's Election Integrity Task Force in 2022 (two years before her first run). Elected At-Large to Hampton City Council Nov 5, 2024 with ~14.81% (3rd of 9 candidates for 3 seats); sworn in Jan 2025; 4-year term ends Dec 31, 2028. Hampton council is officially nonpartisan; Campbell is listed on the Hampton GOP 'Elected Officials' page (hamptongop.org/elected-officials). Campaign self-funded ~74% by spousal loan (~$18k of $24.3k).

carolyn-campbell.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large Hampton City Council member, elected Nov 5, 2024 (~14.81%, 3rd of 9 for 3 seats), sworn in Jan 2025, term ends Dec 31, 2028. Hampton council is officially nonpartisan; Campbell is publicly listed on the Hampton GOP 'Elected Officials' page (party-in-practice Republican-aligned). HARD evidence: (1) LONE NO vote on the May 2025 FY26 Hampton budget — she dissented because the 1-cent real-estate-tax-rate cut to $1.14/$100 didn't go far enough; quote: 'we just have to balance that out with what the people can stomach'; campaign platform explicitly opposes 'unnecessary taxes and fees' — economic_stewardship q2 TRUE (fiscal-restraint posture documented by a roll-call dissent in the tax-cutting direction); (2) explicit campaign-platform statement: 'Hampton must support the parents' right to voice their concerns, allow an open forum for suggestions, and ensure parents that their children are safe...' Daughter of a Hampton police officer; platform states 'Carolyn supports the integrity of the police officers to keep law and order' and 'Policing in our community needs to be supported with full staffing and resources'; voted YES on April 2026 Hampton ordinance banning camping/sleeping on public property (5-2 majority), saying 'I know this ordinance is not perfect, but it's a start' — public_justice q0 TRUE (affirmative pro-LE advocacy bar met with explicit platform + family LE tie + roll-call vote on a public-order ordinance). NULL on family_child_sovereignty q1 — her 'parents' right to voice their concerns' is general parental-voice language, NOT the specific parental notification/consent on medical/mental-health/gender interventions the rubric q1 requires (per the FXBG high-bar discipline: vague slogans → null). NULL on self_defense q0/q1 — Lafayette Gun Club membership + 'law-abiding citizens' rights to self-defense' is supportive 2A rhetoric but not specifically constitutional-carry-without-permit and not specifically opposition to red-flag/mag-limit/AWB/registries (no Moms Demand / Gun Sense endorsement either; that would have been a hard FALSE — she has neither). NULL on election_integrity q1/q4 — Election Integrity Task Force membership and Officer of Election service are activist signals but do not map to specific photo-ID-with-citizenship-verification or anti-Zuckerbucks rubric cells without a direct citable statement. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (Hampton has an LGBTQ+ liaison page and sponsors Phabulous Phoebus Pride but no Campbell-personal vote/statement on a Pride proclamation or marriage), christian_liberty (active Lutheran church involvement is identity, not a specific public-square Christian-symbols / prayer / Sabbath / conscience-rights statement per the high-bar rule), public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4 (no bail-reform / restitution / public-health-framing / sword-of-state statement), refuse_state_overreach (no Dillon-Rule pushback or state-mandate-resistance vote during tenure located), border_immigration (Hampton formally rejected sanctuary label and cooperates with ICE, but that was Mayor Gray's public statement, not Campbell's). Distinguished from Karolyn Campbell (NC, unrelated) and a separate 'Carolyn C. Campbell' candidate elsewhere.

2.note: LONE NO vote on the May 2025 FY26 Hampton budget because the 1-cent real-estate-tax-rate cut to $1.14/$100 didn't go far enough; quote: 'we just have to balance that out with what the people can stomach.' Campaign platform explicitly opposes 'unnecessary taxes and fees' — affirmative fiscal-restraint dissent in the tax-cutting direction.

0.note: Daughter of a Hampton police officer; campaign platform: 'Carolyn supports the integrity of the police officers to keep law and order' and 'Policing in our community needs to be supported with full staffing and resources'; voted YES on April 2026 Hampton ordinance banning camping/sleeping on public property (5-2). Affirmative pro-LE advocacy with concrete platform + roll-call vote.
